title: Rivian Q3 2024 Earnings Analysis
text: Rivian delivered 10,018 vehicles in Q3 2024, representing a 27% decline from Q2's 13,790 units. The company cited supply chain disruptions affecting its R1T, R1S, and electric delivery vans. The shortage stemmed from a miscommunication with its supplier, Essex Furukawa, earlier this year, leaving the manufacturer short on copper windings needed for production targets. Analysts projected approximately $1 billion in revenue—a 26% decrease from Q3 2023's $1.34 billion. Expected loss per share was estimated at $0.96, improving from the prior year's $1.19 loss per share. Rivian adjusted its 2024 production forecast from 57,000 units to 47,000-49,000, though it maintains delivery expectations between 50,500-52,000 vehicles for the year. The company plans to launch its more affordable R2 model (priced at $45,000) in early 2026, aiming to substantially expand market reach and reduce per-unit losses through technological improvements implemented at its Normal, Illinois facility.
